name: New Jersey
examName: New Jersey Real Estate Salesperson Examination

examFacts.notes: Administered by PSI for the New Jersey Real Estate Commission (NJREC), a division of the New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance.

intro:
New Jersey's first-time pass rate runs low, and most candidates who fall short underestimated the 30-question state portion. The New Jersey Real Estate Commission (NJREC) contracts with PSI to administer it as a 110-question test, split into an 80-question national portion and a 30-question New Jersey portion, with four hours to finish. New Jersey grades the salesperson exam as a single combined result rather than scoring each portion on its own, so you need 77 of the 110 questions right overall. That makes New Jersey-specific material the difference between passing and retaking, because the state questions are folded into the one score that decides everything.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. New Jersey piles on the NJREC's licensing and disciplinary powers, the Real Estate Guaranty Fund, the Consumer Fraud Act, the Law Against Discrimination with its broader list of protected classes, the Real Estate Sales Full Disclosure Act and the Real Estate Time Share Act, the Consumer Information Statement that governs how agents disclose whose interests they represent, and the three-business-day attorney-review clause that every residential contract a licensee prepares has to carry. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need New Jersey-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free New Jersey real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full New Jersey bank. Each tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real NJREC practice test, and the ones you miss show you what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the New Jersey question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the New Jersey real estate exam?
faq.a: The New Jersey Real Estate Salesperson Examination has 110 scored multiple-choice questions. PSI builds it from an 80-question national portion and a 30-question New Jersey portion, and you may also see a handful of unscored pretest items mixed in. The questions are intermixed in a single sitting rather than given as two separate tests.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the New Jersey real estate exam?
faq.a: 70%, which is 77 of the 110 scored questions correct. New Jersey grades the salesperson exam as one combined score across the national and state questions, so unlike the broker exam there is no separate per-portion bar to clear. PSI tells you whether you passed before you leave the test center.

faq.q: How much does the New Jersey real estate exam cost?
faq.a: The salesperson examination fee is $45 per attempt, paid to PSI when you schedule. You can sit it at one of PSI's New Jersey test centers or through online proctoring. That fee is separate from the license fee you'll pay the New Jersey Real Estate Commission after you pass.

faq.q: How long is the New Jersey real estate exam?
faq.a: Four hours total for the salesperson exam. That single block covers all 110 questions, the 80 national and the 30 New Jersey, and you find out whether you passed before you leave the test center.

faq.q: Who administers the New Jersey real estate exam?
faq.a: The New Jersey Real Estate Salesperson Examination is administered by PSI for the New Jersey Real Estate Commission (NJREC). You register and schedule your exam date through PSI.

faq.q: Is the New Jersey real estate exam hard?
faq.a: New Jersey's first-time pass rate runs low, and most candidates who fail underestimate the state portion. The 80 national questions are the same shared content candidates see everywhere, and a decent prep course gets you there. The 30 New Jersey questions are where people lose. You are tested on the NJREC's powers and the Real Estate Guaranty Fund, the Consumer Fraud Act and the Law Against Discrimination, the Real Estate Sales Full Disclosure Act and the Time Share Act, the Consumer Information Statement on agency, and the three-business-day attorney-review clause that every residential contract has to carry. Honest practice on New Jersey-specific material is the difference between passing on your first $45 attempt and retaking.

faq.q: What's on the New Jersey real estate exam?
faq.a: The 80-question national portion covers property ownership and land use, valuation and market analysis, financing and mortgages, agency and fiduciary duties, contracts, transfer of title, leasing and property management, fair housing, and real estate math. The 30-question New Jersey portion tests the Real Estate Commission's duties and powers, the statutes and rules governing licensee conduct under Title 45 and N.J.A.C. 11:5, the Real Estate Guaranty Fund, the New Jersey Real Estate Sales Full Disclosure Act, the New Jersey Law Against Discrimination, the New Jersey Real Estate Time Share Act, the Consumer Information Statement that sets out the agency relationships a licensee can offer, and New Jersey practice rules on advertising, trust accounts, commissions, and the attorney-review clause.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the New Jersey real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish your 75-hour pre-license course, the highest-leverage thing you can do is grind through New Jersey-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. National practice tests will help you on the 80 national questions but will not catch you on the Guaranty Fund, the Consumer Fraud Act, the Law Against Discrimination, the Full Disclosure Act, or the Consumer Information Statement. The RealReady app gives you the full New Jersey question bank plus the shared national bank, a missed-question drill mode, and per-category progress tracking so you can see exactly where the state portion is leaking points. Most users study 30 to 60 minutes a day for 2 to 4 weeks before sitting the PSI exam.

The 20 New Jersey practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the New Jersey bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the New Jersey real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated New Jersey question bank built for the New Jersey real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the New Jersey law that trips people up, like the Real Estate Guaranty Fund, the Consumer Information Statement, and the attorney-review clause.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward New Jersey's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
